title: clematis helenae
intro: Clematis helenae, also known as Helen's Clematis, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Clematis helenae include its ability to improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Clematis helenae can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for its medicinal purposes.
